name: Chipotle Cream Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tsp Cornstarch dissolved in 2 tsp water
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to boil. Add chiles and boil until tender, about 15 minutes.
2. Drain the chiles, reserving 1/4 cup cooking liquid. Puree the chiles with the reserved cooking liquid in a blender.
3. Strain through a sieve. Set aside.
4. Melt butter in a heavy large skillet over medium heat. Add 1 tablespoon chipotle paste, onion, rosemary, pepper, shallot, and garlic, and stir for 2 minutes.
5. Add white wine and boil until reduced by half. Add cream and boil until reduced to a thin sauce consistency, stirring occasionally, about 10 minutes.
6. Taste and add more chipotle paste if desired.
7. Add the cornstarch mixture and boil for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
(Note: This recipe can be prepared up to one day in advance. Allow it to cool completely before covering and refrigerating. Reheat before using. It makes approximately 3-3/4 cups. Serve with Crab Enchiladas.)
